title: Stelis nivalis
taxonomy: Stelis nivalis is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Stelis, which is a group of small to medium-sized orchids. Stelis nivalis is further classified into the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a large and diverse group within the orchid family. Within the subtribe, Stelis nivalis is also classified into the tribe Epidendroideae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this species within the orchid family.

intro: Stelis nivalis, also known as Winter-flowering Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the high-altitude regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Stelis nivalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis nivalis is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat various respiratory conditions, as well as to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
